title: castilleja lanata subsp. hololeuca
intro: Castilleja lanata subsp. hololeuca, also known as Holodendrum or Mountain Indian Paintbrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Castilleja lanata subsp. hololeuca include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and soothing respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal properties, although its use and efficacy may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
